titleStmt.title: SII 23.42: original edition by G.V. Srinivasa Rao
No. 42. (A.R. No. 42 of 1907.) Tiruviśalūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District Sivayōganātha temple—on the same wall Rājarāja I: year 29: 1013-14 A.D.

summary.p: A slight deviation is found in the historical introduction in that it adds the “Twelve Thousand Islands” to the usual list of the king's conquests. The reference is evidently to the Maldives which are said to have been known by that name in ancient tradition, and which the king should have conquered late in his reign. (Prof. K.A.N. Sastri's ‘The Coḷas’. Vol. I, p. 220).

The inscription registers an agreement given by the mahāsabhā of Vēmbaṟṟūr alias Śōḻamārttāṇḍa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam a brahmadēya in Maṇṇi-nāḍu, to the temple of Tiruviśalūr-Mahādēva. A sum of 458 kāśu was endowed to the temple and deposited with the assembly by queen Dantiśaktiviṭaṅkiyār alias Lōkamahādēviyar on the occasion of the performance of tulābhāra by the king and of hiraṇyagarbha by herself in the temple. As interest on this amount they undertook to measure out annually 229 kalam of paddy at the rate of 1 tūṇi and 1 padakku on every kāśu per year, and with this paddy the ūr-vāriyam and kuḍumbu committees of the year were to meet the daily requirements of akkāraḍalai offering to the god, of which the details are given as follows:
Rice—2 nāḻi .. .. .. .. One kuruṇi and 2 nāḻi
Earthen pot .. .. .. .. 1 nāḻi, 1 uri and 2(3/4) śeviḍu
Remuneration to person preparing the offering .. 1 nāḻi

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti śrī . tirumakaḷpola perunilaccelviyum tanakke Urimai pūṇṭamai maṉa
kkoḷak kāntaḷurccālai kala maṟuttaruḷi veṅkaināṭum kaṅkapāṭiyum nuḷampa
pāṭiyum taṭikaipāṭiyum kuṭamalaināṭum kollamum kaliṅkamum muraṭ ṭoḻiṟ ciṅkaḷar Īḻa
maṇṭalamum Iraṭṭapāṭi Eḻarai Ilakkamum muṉṉirp paḻan=tivu paṉṉi rāyiramum tiṇṭiṟal veṉṟi
t taṇṭāṟk koṇṭa taṉneḻil vaḷar Ūḻiyuḷ ḷellāyāṇṭu n=toḻutaka viḷaṅkum yāṇṭe ceḻiyarai
tecukoḷ śrī kovirājarājakeśarivanmarāṉa śrī [rājarā]jatevaṟkku yāṇṭu Irupattoṉpatāvatu Irāje
ntraciṅkavaḷanāṭṭu maṇṇināṭṭu brahmadeyam vempaṟṟūrākiya coḻa māttāṇṭa catur
vetimaṅkalattu mahāsabhaiyom kaiyeḻuttu . nammai Uṭaiya cakkira vartti Uṭaiyār śrīrājarā
jadevar Ivvūr tiruvicalūr māhādevar śrīkoyililey tulāpāram pukkaruḷiṉa Aṉṟu nampirā
ṭṭiyār tan=ticattiviṭaṅkiyārāṉa Ulokamādeviyār Iraṇyagarbham puk karuḷi Ittiruvicalūr māhadevarkku
Akkāraṭalai Amutukku veṇṭum nivan=taṅkaḷuk cu 458 Ikkācu ṉāṉūṟṟuaimpat
teṭṭum Ittiruvicalūr mahādevar senāpatikaḷ mulabhṛtyarāṉa caṇḍaṭeśvaradevarpakkal māhāsabhai
yom koṇṭa Ikkācu nāṉūṟṟu Aimpatteṭṭum koṇṭu kaṭavom koṇṭa paricāvatu cittirai
mutal kācu 1- kku palicai Āṭṭaiviṭṭaṉ nellu tūṇipatakkāka Ikkā
cukku Orāṭṭaip palicaiyāl van=ta nellu 2 100 2 10 9 � Iṉ nellu Iru nūṟṟiru
(iru)pattu Oṉpatiṉ kalattukkum Ivakkāraṭalai Amutukku veṇṭum
nivan=tam maṟupaṭāc cenṉel Arici Irunāḻiyiṉukku nellu Aiñ ñāḻikku pari
iraṭṭi nellu kuṟuṇi Irunāḻiyum tuparuppu nāḻiyiṉukku nittam nellu Aiññāḻiyum
pacuviṉpāl ṉāṉāḻikku nittam nellu patakkum naṟuney Uḻakkinukku nittam ṟu
ṇiyum caṟkkarai paṉṉirupalavaraikku nittam nellu kuṟuṇi Iruṉāḻi yum vāḻaip tinuk
ku nittam nellu Aṟuṉāḻiyum Aṭaikkāyamutukku veṟuṅkāy pattukkum Ilaiamutukku kove
ṟṟilai ṉāṟppatiṉukkumāka nitta Irunāḻi Uriyum tiruvamutu Aṭa kucak kalam putuppāṉaikkum
nemikkumāka kucavaṉukku nittam nellu nāṭuriyey Iraṇṭemukkāl ceviṭum tiruvamutu Aṭa veṇṭum viṟa
kukku nittam nellu nāḻiyum Akkāraṭalait tiruvamutu Aṭum māṇikku nittam nellu nāḻiyummāka nit
tam nellu Eḻukuṟuṇi Aiññāḻiyum Iraṇṭemukkālc ceviṭāka Orāṭṭainā
ḷaikku nellu Irunūṟṟirupattoṉpatiṉ kalamum cantirā(ta)tittavaṟ
Ivva ṇam palicai Aṭṭi nimantam celuttakkaṭa
vomākavum nimantam celuttum paricu Āṭṭaiviṭ
ṭaṉum Avvāṇṭu Ūrvāriyañ ceyvārum
kuṭumpu ceyvāru variiley nilavaca
m vari koṇṭu Inellu taṇṭuvittu tukaṟpatar cettalStops here.
Digital edition of SII 23.42 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
